perturbation.original_text: "Confidential Information" shall mean, as to any Party as a Recipient, (a) all non-public written, graphic, oral, electromagnetic or other information (including technical, financial and business information regarding models, market studies, business plans. clients' names, reports, plans. projections, data or any other information) relating to the business, strategy, prospects or plans of the other Party or any of the other Party's Affiliates and (b) any reproductions, copies, notes, analyses, work papers. compilations, studies, interpretations or any other document or information derived from any of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: "Confidential Information" shall mean, as to any Party as a Recipient, (a) all non-public written, graphic, oral, electromagnetic or other information (including technical, financial and business information regarding models, market studies, business plans. clients' names, reports, plans. projections, data or any other information) relating to the business, strategy, prospects or plans of the other Party or any of the other Party's Affiliates and (b) any reproductions, copies, notes, analyses, work papers. compilations, studies, interpretations or any other document or information derived from any of the foregoing. However, information that is readily available on the internet or through public channels is not considered Confidential Information for the purposes of this agreement, regardless of its source.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Confidential Information' is broad and includes almost all non-public information. The addition of 'However, information that is readily available on the internet or through public channels is not considered Confidential Information for the purposes of this agreement, regardless of its source' creates a contradiction. It introduces ambiguity about the scope of confidentiality, since data originally considered non-public can later be public, and that affects the obligations related to protecting that data under Article IV.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 4.1 Obligation to Maintain and Protect Confidentiality. The Recipient agrees that it shall receive, protect and maintain the Confidential Information in the strictest confidence. The Recipient agrees to keep all of the Confidential Information confidential, not to use that Confidential Information other than for the purpose of this Agreement and not to disclose the same to any Person other than in accordance with this Agreement without the prior written consent of the Party from whom such Confidential Information was received, unless: (a) the Recipient is required to make the disclosure by Law or under any administrative guideline or directive (whether or not having the force of Law) the observance of which, if not having the force of Law, is in accordance with the practice of responsible persons similarly situated; (b) the disclosure is necessary for the purpose of obtaining any consent, authorization, approval or license from any Government Authority with respect to the transactions effected pursuant to this Agreement; (c) it is necessary that the disclosure be made to any taxation or fiscal authority; (d) the disclosure is made in accordance with Section 4.3 to the officers, employees, or agents of the Recipient, or to the professional advisors of the Recipient for the purpose of obtaining professional advice in relation to this Agreement or any matter the subject thereof or otherwise for the purpose of consulting those professional advisors; (e) the disclosure is necessary or desirable in relation to any procedure for discovery of documents and any proceedings before any court, tribunal (including any arbitral tribunal) or regulatory body; or In respect of any Confidential Information to be disclosed by the Recipient pursuant to the exceptions referred to in this Section 4.1, the Recipient must, prior to such disclosure, notify the Party from whom the Confidential Information was received of the need or intention to disclose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the definition of 'Confidential Information,' excluding information readily available on the internet or through public channels. This directly contradicts Section 4.1, which obligates the recipient to maintain and protect 'Confidential Information' in the strictest confidence. If information initially considered confidential becomes publicly available, the obligation to protect it under Section 4.1 becomes unclear and potentially unenforceable, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The change alters the scope of what is considered confidential and therefore protected.

perturbation.original_text: Section 5.3 Term and Termination. This Agreement is effective on the Effective Date and shah continue in effect until the earlier of (a) the fifth (5t h) anniversary of the Effective Date, and (b) termination by either Party for any reason upon thirty (30) days' written notice to the other Party; provided, however, that no Party shall be able to terminate this Agreement so long as such Party is a Defaulting Party under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 5.3 Term and Termination. This Agreement is effective on the Effective Date and shah continue in effect until the earlier of (a) the fifth (5t h) anniversary of the Effective Date, and (b) termination by either Party for any reason upon thirty (30) days' written notice to the other Party; provided, however, that no Party shall be able to terminate this Agreement so long as such Party is a Defaulting Party under this Agreement. Notwithstanding the forgoing, Lloyds may terminate this agreement at anytime, without notice or liability to Lightbridge.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that either party can terminate with 30 days notice, except if they are a Defaulting Party. The added clause 'Notwithstanding the forgoing, Lloyds may terminate this agreement at anytime, without notice or liability to Lightbridge.' creates a direct contradiction. It gives Lloyds an unconditional right to terminate, regardless of whether they are a Defaulting Party or have given notice, while the first clause establishes a condition to terminate. This creates uncertainty about the actual termination rights of the parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 5.3 Term and Termination. This Agreement is effective on the Effective Date and shah continue in effect until the earlier of (a) the fifth (5t h) anniversary of the Effective Date, and (b) termination by either Party for any reason upon thirty (30) days' written notice to the other Party; provided, however, that no Party shall be able to terminate this Agreement so long as such Party is a Defaulting Party under this Agreement. Notwithstanding the forgoing, Lloyds may terminate this agreement at anytime, without notice or liability to Lightbridge.
perturbation.justification: The added clause in the changed text grants Lloyds the unilateral right to terminate the agreement 'at anytime, without notice or liability to Lightbridge.' This directly contradicts the original clause, which requires 30 days' written notice for termination by either party and prohibits termination by a Defaulting Party. The original and contradicted text states: 'termination by either Party for any reason upon thirty (30) days' written notice to the other Party; provided, however, that no Party shall be able to terminate this Agreement so long as such Party is a Defaulting Party under this Agreement.' while the added clause states: 'Notwithstanding the forgoing, Lloyds may terminate this agreement at anytime, without notice or liability to Lightbridge.' This creates a structural flaw and a clear in-text contradiction as Lloyds is given preferential treatment.

perturbation.original_text: Section 7.5 Assignment. No Party may assign this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 7.5 Assignment. No Party may assign this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Party. However, Lightbridge may assign this agreement to any of its affiliates without prior written consent.
perturbation.explanation: The original text prohibits assignment without prior written consent from the other party. The addition of 'However, Lightbridge may assign this agreement to any of its affiliates without prior written consent.' introduces a contradiction. This creates ambiguity and uncertainty regarding assignment restrictions within the text. Since this creates a special condition, it can affect the rights of Lloyds based on what happens at Section 7.4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 7.4 Binding Effect. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the Parties and their respective successors and permitted assigns.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 7.5 states that 'No Party may assign this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Party.' The changed text adds an exception: 'However, Lightbridge may assign this agreement to any of its affiliates without prior written consent.' This directly contradicts the initial restriction on assignment. Section 7.4 states that the agreement 'shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the Parties and their respective successors and permitted assigns.' The changed text in Section 7.5, by allowing Lightbridge to assign the agreement to its affiliates without consent, potentially alters the 'permitted assigns' and therefore contradicts the binding effect provision in Section 7.4. This qualifies as a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction.
